what: The installed MACRO ADDONS as a reference source - a SECOND attested-usage channel. The client's OWN code yields ZERO conditional literals; addon code yields a few.
NOT_A_FACT_SOURCE: Nothing here is a fact about the CoA client. Downloaded != working. An addon shipping a macro string proves its AUTHOR BELIEVED it works on their target client - one notch stronger than a wiki (it is code aimed at a client), still not proof. Do not cite reference/ as basis/.
standing_note: Battlewrath, 2026-07-17: 'Whilst they aren't verified as true, they might frame the validation better.'
corroborates_the_C_wall: SuperDuperMacro's entire job is interpreting macros, and its MacroInterpreter.lua - explicitly 'adapted from ChatFrame.lua' - STILL calls SecureCmdOptionParse instead of reimplementing conditionals. An author who NEEDED the vocabulary in Lua could not get it either. INDEPENDENT support for the central finding: the vocabulary is C-side and no source read reaches it.
not_a_command_mirror: SuperDuperMacro's funcsToGetSpell mirrors only the 8 commands that YIELD a spell/item (it needs the icon), NOT the command surface. Diffing it against basis/commands.json produced a bogus 41-command 'delta' that was SCOPE, not a finding. Recorded so the mistake is not repeated.
noise_note: A regex over `[...]` cannot tell a macro clause from LUA TABLE INDEXING. First run 'found' [group], [button] and [target] - every one was sdm_exclusiveGroups[group] / AceConsole.weakcommands[target]. A clause now counts ONLY inside a string literal AND with a known conditional head.
